title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied sentenced for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts, including skulls and skins, from 2018 to 2021.
Bied will serve eight months in prison, two years supervised release, and pay a $75,000 fine for wildlife trafficking.
Bied smuggled animal parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, disguising them as 'decorative masks' and 'rodents' to avoid detection.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black was convicted of first-degree murder for fatally stabbing Milteer Hendricks in Falmouth in 2023.
Black, 22 at the time, and four others drove to an apartment with the intention of attacking Hendricks, prosecutors said.
Hendricks was stabbed in the chest and died the following day; Black was arrested two days post-incident.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia suspended with pay by Massachusetts State Police after being named in an investigative report.
Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide; involved in a fatal crash while allegedly intoxicated.
DeLucia allegedly advised keeping Quigley's suspected intoxication information secret, according to Middlesex DA's investigation.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter in the death of their foster child.
The 10-month-old foster child died in 2020 from treatable conditions due to neglected medical care in their custody.
Sentences staggered due to other children; Tucker and Barlow-Tucker barred from caregiving roles outside their family.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was sentenced to life without parole for fatally poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder for poisoning Fowler's Powerade drink, motivated by jealousy over an affair.
Fowler died from ethylene glycol poisoning; Church filed an appeal but was implicated by DNA on a tainted bottle.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox started the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, struggling in pitching and defense.
Boston's pitchers collectively have a 5.22 ERA and are last in MLB for home runs allowed per nine innings.
The Red Sox batting lineup is underperforming with a .208 average, ranking 29th in on-base percentage.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, announced his NFL retirement after 13 seasons via Instagram, expressing gratitude for his career.
Gilmore played for six teams, including Buffalo and four seasons with the Patriots, winning Super Bowl LIII.
He earned five Pro Bowl selections, a Defensive Player of the Year award, and recorded 32 career interceptions.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle has started global layoffs, with termination notices issued to employees as part of organizational changes.
Analysts estimate layoffs could affect 20,000 to 30,000 workers, potentially 18% of Oracle's workforce of 162,000.
Massachusetts' WARN report as of March 27 shows no Oracle layoffs, affecting offices in Burlington, Mass. and Nashua, N.H.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year contract with the Cincinnati Bengals after playing with the Steelers last season.
Dugger was traded to the Steelers for a sixth-round pick and had 42 tackles in nine games with them.
The former Patriots captain played all 17 games in 2023, recording 109 tackles and seven pass breakups.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Boxborough family returned from vacation to find their truck stolen from Logan Airport's central parking garage.
Massachusetts State Police found the truck had exited the garage via 'piggybacking' behind another vehicle.
State Police noted similarities with a prior truck theft from the same garage; both cases are under investigation.
